entry.title: Re: Dimension data access control & Admin authorization -...
entry.content:
@Deb_D 

Roles take precedence over any Data Access Control, so if a User has a Role which has “Full Data Access” enabled, such as “BI Admin” or “Admin”, then this will take priority over any Data Access Control set on any Dimension or Public Dimension used within a Model. So yes its your Admin Rights which is superseding your DA setup. Try removing your Admin rights, or test with another user with lesser privilege roles.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Project ID can be Automatic encoding base on project type?
entry.content:
Hi ALL,

In APP Project Control - Enterprise Projects , I create a project.

Project ID can be Automatic encoding base on project type? or base on Project Profile ?

entry.title: Re: Selection text not getting deleted in report
entry.content:
Basically if a parameter or select-options is removed from program, first time you dispklay the selection-text tab a pop-up asking to remove those deleted value shoud arise, strange that you didn't get it?

  • Can you try this option in program text menu: Utilities, Adjust, Selection Texts

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content: Hi @joncheng97, thank you for your reply. This is a new system to get support from the Learning team, and as mentioned on the blog the Support agents are available 24 hours per day, Monday to Friday and most queries are responded to within 24 hours. If you wish to have a call, you can request that when creating your case.

entry.title: SAP Customer Data Cloud Dataflow Custom Script
entry.content:
While working with SAP Customer Data Cloud Dataflow Custom Script. What is way to move to the next record inside record.evaluate component. Let me share my expectation,

If record.profile.firstName != "Jack", Then the current record value will be assigned to the next record value. Suppose there are three records: record1, record2, record3. If the record1 does not have Jack in first name, then I want to move to record2 (i.e. record = record2).

I have followed the same steps as you mentioned above.

In my trail account I have created two subaccount. In one subaccount I am having hana instance and in second subaccount I am trying to access the hana instance from first subaccount. I have created instance mapping as well and did the required changes in mta.yaml

At the time of deploying an application to second subaccount I am getting below error:

Error staging application "XYZ": NoCompatibleCell - Found no compatible cell

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI/ Integration Suite Splitter returning as repo...

entry.title: Bank statement Automation CAMT.053 and CAMT.052
entry.content:
As per ISO20022 standard, by end of 2025 text format will be replaced with XML formats. Bank statement format MT940 will be transitioned to XML format CAMT.053. Usage of Payment Order option for scenarios such as

  • Non-Functional currency Payment from Functional currency bank account. How exchange rate fluctuation resulting in exchange gain/loss can be avoided
  • Supply Chain Financing: - Vendor payments are pledged with bank with agreed days
  • Customer Direct Debit : - Delay in credit on bank statement

 

User Story

Priyanaka is responsible for process payments for both domestic and cross border payments. However, the payment to be processed is a functional currency bank account. For example, when payment for non-functional currency payment is processed on 22-Nov-2024 with a value date as 25-Nov-2024. Since, the exchange rate on 22-Nov-2024 and 25-Nov-2024 are different when bank statement is received on 26-Nov-24, system was not able to clear the open items. Due to the exchange rate difference Credit to clearing bank account and debit to clearing bank account are different value in local currency.

She had also express difficulty on Customer direct debit. As customer direct debit will have a lead time in crediting on bank statement. Whereas, customer outstanding is reported incorrectly / cash flow report is incorrect. As we have credited customer and Cash flow forecast is impacted as money is yet to be received.

 

Business Requirement

  1. When a payment is processed for non-functional currency payment on 22-Nov-2024 and debited on 26-Nov-2024, instead of exchange rate gain/loss system should be able to clear open item automatically.
  2. As there is a change in ISO20022 standard in Nov-2025 text formats will be replaced with XML formats. Transition from MT940 to XML bank statement is required.
  3. Another challenge, bank is unable to provide payment details on End of the day bank statements. However, bank can give item wise payment details in another statement file.
  4. Customer direct debit, cash flow to be reported as forecasted until money is credited on bank statement

SAP Solution

ISO20022 standard transition from MT940 to XML. New XML bank statements format are CAMT.053.001.02 EOD summary and CAMT.052.001.02 is detailed line-item statements. SAP ECC version or S4 HANA version is compatible to meet the transition into XML bank statements.

Payment order Solution: -

Payment Order means that the payment program does not post a payment document and the paid items are not cleared. Instead, the system generates and saves a payment order. The information contained in this order can be used to clear the items later. The payment is posted when it appears on the bank statement, and the relevant open items are selected when the payment order is entered. The paid items remain blocked for other clearing transactions and further payment runs until the payment has been posted.

End of the day bank statement CAMT.053.001.02 will have a summary of payment orders debited on 26-Nov-2024 with exchange rate applicable on 26-Nov-24. Itemized bank statement CAMT.053.002.01 with item wise payment orders received on 26-Nov-24 with exchange rate applicable on 26-Nov-24.

When CAMT.052 is processed, customer open item will be posted without impacting the realized exchange rate gain/loss.

Customization are required are

  • Payment method to be classified as payment order
  • Define Posting rules to debit vendor and clear open item
  • External transaction assignment to Posting rule classified with interpretation algorithm 029- Payment Order Number

Payment method setup

Account symbol setup

Posting Rules setup

External Transaction determination in CAMT.053

External Transaction type -> <Domn> <Cd> + <Fmly><Cd> +<SubFmlyCd>

External Transaction type - PMNTICDTPMOR

Configuration and testing CAMT.053 and CAMT.053

https://1drv.ms/v/s!Ah2KZic1YUA_gZpe6apDUeyPmTgagQ?e=bywXSI  

Note: While processing bank statement example FF_5 t-code, enter BELNR number interval and XBLNR number interval. Payment order clear requires algorithms selection of BELNR & XBLNR number intervals. If we don't update these fields while bank statement processing, Payment order might not post.

Disclaimer

Please note this solution works if bank is sending consolidated amount on CAMT.053 and provide payments itemize in CAMT.052 statement. Family, sub family on CAMT files are only for demo purpose. Please check Family, sub family codes received on CAMT file from bank.

Conclusion

Payment order and CAMT.053, CAMT.052 solutions can be extended to following business scenarios. If bank statement formats are in text, MT940 as EOD statement and MT942 can be used

  • Supply Chain Finance: - Depending on business requirement and bank statements (XML)
  • Customer direct debit: - Customer direct debits processed are credited on bank statement (XML) on later days (T+2 days)
  • Cross border payments from local currency bank account to avoid exchange gain/loss

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2411 Release – What’s New?
entry.content:
The 2411 release went live recently. It has been a major release with many interesting features. This blog post gives you an overview of the release highlights including:

  • a new version of the ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’ application in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration
  • a new way to define email rules and
  • the option to share shipment details via a public link, both in SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace.

 Please note, the blog is intended for shippers and solution owners, carriers, and data providers as well as sales and presales colleagues who already have a detailed understanding of SAP Business Network for Logistics. 

So let’s go through all those new features.

SAP Business Network for Logistics – Connectivity

Reminder: Sunset of SAP BTP Neo environment, switch to BTP Multi-Cloud environment

The integration platform of SAP Business Network for Logistics is running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), Neo environment. The Neo environment has been deprecated and will be sunset by December 31, 2025. A new integration platform based on SAP BTP Multi-Cloud environment is now available.

There are configuration changes required in SAP Business Network for Logistics as well as in your backend systems. The configuration apps in SAP Business Network for Logistics are now updated to accommodate these changes.

You can find our migration guides here:

  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Shippers
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Carriers
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Solution Owners
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Data Contributors

Destination URLs and OAuth 2.0 token URLs for ‘Provider API for Order Events’ updated

With the integration platform migration, the destination URLs and OAuth 2.0 token URLs for Provider API for Order Events (GTT and FC were updated. When you configure the environment for trying out APIs in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you will find the new URLs and endpoints in the following fields:

  • Starting URL: select the URL based on your data centre.
  • Resulting URL: updated upon your selection of the starting URL.
  • Token URL: updated upon your selection of the endpoint.

Endpoint: select the endpoint based on your data centre.

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Contracting: ‘Freight Order for Confirmation’ – new app version for carriers

The application ‘Freight Orders for Conformation’ can be used by carriers to confirm or reject freight orders send by the shipper. A new version of this application has been launched.

When you first log onto the application after this release, you will get the following pop up.

 Later, if you also change to the other version (see picture below).

 The new version of the application comes with the following changes:

  • Freight order status have been updated (reduced from 9 to 7)
  • New filter added ‘Response Required’
  • Modified worklist tabs
  • Under the worklist ‘New’, there are now subtabs ‘All’ and ‘Updated’
  • Get a message in the worklist, when a freight order has been updated
  • View when a freight order has been updated in the ‘Freight Order Status’ column
  • Introduction of ‘Order Categorization’ (based on statuses) to identify updated orders and status 

 

 

Contracting: ‘Freight Requests for Quotations’ app – new filters

In the ‘Freight Requests for Quotations’ app, the following new fields have been added in the filter and worklist area:

  • Requested Loading Start Date Time
  • Requested Loading End Date Time
  • Acceptable Loading Start End Time
  • Acceptable Loading End Date Time

In the worklist area, the ‘Requested Loading Duration’ field has been added.

Execution: ‘Freight Orders for Reporting’ app – reporting enabled while freight order needs reconfirmation

In the ‘Freight Orders for Reporting’ app, carriers can now report events for a freight order even when they have not reconfirmed the freight order (after it has been updated by the shipper).

There is a new filter ‘Orders Pending Reconfirmation’ to select the respective orders and a message appears in the worklist to notify about updated freight orders. There is also a new tag ‘Updated’ that indicates updated freight orders in the worklist.

 

 

Please note: If reporting has already been completed and a freight order is updated by the shipper, the reporting status “Completed” remains.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

‘Manage Models’ application: new email condition configuration tool

Solution owners can now define rules for email notifications on a configuration UI. Compared with defining rules in an event-to-action script, it’s easier and more convenient.

You can access this configuration UI by clicking ‘Email Rules’ on the model card in the Manage Models app. From there, you can:

  • create rules and define the rule settings,
  • import and export rules,
  • activate and deactivate rules.

 

 

After a rule is processed and all conditions are met, an email notification is sent to the designated recipient(s) automatically. For more, see Define the Rule on the Configuration UI in How to Send Notifications.

There is also a detailed deck on this new feature as well as a demo that you can find on the release readiness web page.

‘Manage Models’ application: Define semantic colours for custom execution status

In the ‘Manage Models’ app, you can now define semantic colours for custom execution status of shipments and freight units. The status of the following categories are shown in different colours:

  • Success (Green)
  • Inactive (Grey)
  • Error (Red)
  • Warning (Yellow)
  • Information (Blue)

This helps you distinguish between different categories of statuses and assess the situation for timely and appropriate decision making. For more, see Define My Execution Status.

‘Manage Event Logs’ application: Enhanced auto-retrigger rules

In the ‘Manage Event Logs’ app, the following new fields were added on the auto-retrigger rule configuration pop-up:

  • Postpone First Retrigger
  • Retrigger Frequency

This allows solution owners more flexibility to configure auto-retrigger rules for reprocessing messages.

For more, see Auto Retrigger Rules in Guide for Message Log Administrators.

‘Manage Message Logs’: Save personalized filter and table settings as a tile

On the worklist page, you can now save the personalized filter and table settings as a tile.

This provides easy and quick access to the data you want to see. You don’t have to apply filters and adjust table settings again. For more, see Save a Tile in the Guide for Log Administrators.

‘Track Shipments’ application: Share shipment details via public link

Solution owners can now share shipment details with business partners by generating a public link. With the link, business partners can view shipment details on a simplified ‘Shipment Detail’s page without the need for authentication and sign-in credentials. This easy access facilitates the logistical collaboration between stakeholders.

The information is only an extract of the shipment information based on the shipment details in the ‘Track Shipments’ application and is read only. For example, external code lists (e.g. for shipment status) are utilized, the tracking timeline only shows actual events and internal documents are hidden as well as mobile numbers.

Before you can share, you must enable generating public links of shipments in the event-to-action script in the ‘Manage Models’ app. Please note: the functionality is disabled per default.

For more, see Share Shipment Details via a Public Link in Guide for Transportation Planners.

There is also a detailed deck on this new feature that you can find on the release readiness web page.

‘Track Shipments’ application: Display user-defined tracking unit information

You can view user-defined information for tracking units in the:

  • Tracking units table on the ‘Shipment Details’ and ‘Freight Unit Details’ pages or
  • On the ‘Information’ tab on the ‘Tracking Unit Details’ page. (via the ‘Adapt UI’ function).

 

‘Track Shipments’ application: new filters

Solution Owners can now filter shipments by ‘Estimated Departure Date Time’ and ‘Actual Arrival Time’ and see the information in the corresponding columns of the worklist.

 

On the details page, you can also display the fields on the header or in the Information tab with the ‘Adapt UI’ function.

All Fulfillment Tracking applications: New event reason code

The event reason code TRACKING_END (Tracking End) is now supported. It indicates the tracking of the tracked process is ended and no events can be reported to it further.

 

Pease share and amplify our new features!

---------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2411. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2411 release can be found in the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: I want to fix the IP address of BTP.
entry.content:
We are calling an external SaaS from a BTP application (Cloud Foundry).
The external SaaS needs to be IP address restricted.
So we want to fix the IP address of BTP.
Is there a way to do this?

Best regards.

entry.title: SOLMAN_SETUPにおける、Wily Introscope未導入の影響範囲に関して
entry.content:
ご担当者様


いつもお世話になっております。


掲題の件について、今回新環境の要件としてWily Introscopeは未導入の状態でSolutionManagerを構築しております。
これに伴い、SOLMAN_SETUPにおける「管理対象システム設定 」にて、以下の設定のステータスが赤信号になっております。
これらはWily Introscope未導入による影響と判断して問題ないかをご教示願います。


　【ABAPシステム】
　　　・Introscopeホストアダプタ
　【JAVAシステム】
　　　・Introscopeホストアダプタ
　　　・バイトコードアダプタのインストール
　　　・システムレベルメトリクス生成


以上になります、よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SOLMAN_SETUPにおける、Wily Introscope未導入の影響範囲に関して
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

はい、ご認識の通りです。

｢Introscopeホストアダプタ｣と｢バイトコードアダプタのインストール｣はCA Introscopeデータコレクションの一部であり、

前提条件として、Enter System Parametersステップでご選択されるIntroscope Enterprise Manager が起動している必要がございます。

アクティビティ｢システムレベルメトリクス生成｣は、システムおよびインスタンスレベルでパフォーマンスメトリクスを集約するために Introscope Enterprise Manager を構成するためのものでございます。

以上、ご不明な点等ございましたらお知らせください。

宜しくお願いいたします。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Explore the Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
🎉 There is an update available for the Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance 🎉

Please find more information in the newest blog post.

entry.title: Re: Service instances created in BTP after CAP Application deployment on Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
It depends on the application which services are required. However, I'll try to give you an overview on your created services.

  • Destination Service: You need this if you want to access resources via a destination, e.g. a SAP System via destination + Cloud Connector. If your app does not access any external resources you might omit this.
  • XSUAA: You most likely need this as this enables authorization.
  • HTML5 Repository Service: This depends on your setup. There's another way to serve your HTML5 which does not require the service.
  • HDI Containers: It is odd that you have two of these. Is this intentional? The HDI container represents your database (access).

entry.title: Re: How to add the restriction on APP: Product List
entry.content:
Hello,

I received the following response from the experts:

Currently, this is not possible.

Can we please ask you to raise your request on the Customer Influence Portal? We continuously review and prioritize feature requests based on customer feedback. 

Thank you very much!

Best regards,

Henrike

entry.title: AIF Runtime configuration group for non XML interfaces
entry.content:
Hi There,

We have multiple interfaces ( XML,IDOC,PROXY..etc) under the name space and now we would like to create 'RUN TIME CONFIG GROUP ' to improve AIF performance. Can i create a RTG with different engines ?

If yes please let me know how can we create it.

Thanks in Advance  🙂

entry.title: electronic bank statement - plausibility checks for upload
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we are currently working on a ECC 6.0 and I would like to know, if it is possible in SAP standard to implement plausiblity checks for the upload of the EBS. e.g.:

- is a warning/error possible, if the same bank statement no. for the bank account is already existing in the system? Currently, the system just skips the upload for the statement without warning.

- is a error/warning possible, if the statements are not uploaded in consecuitve sequence. If statement no. 50 is missing in the system, a warning should be displayed, if the next statement no. 51 is uploaded

- is a check possible, if the opening balance of the uploaded statement doesn´t match the ending balance of the previous statement? For this check there is already the system message FB772 available. Is it enough to simply activate the message in table T100S or is there further configuration necessary?

- are there other possible checks to help with data quality?

many thanks in advance,

Hedwig

entry.title: Re: Error in learning journey content / designing asset a...
entry.content: Hi, this must be a problem with the cache from your browser. Can you try with a different browser or with the private browsing mode if your browser? If you are still having problems, you would need to contact the support https://support.learning.sap.com/#/

entry.content:
Hello @wsamuel,

Did you find a solution for your problem ? I have the same issue with a replication flow from S/4HANA in intial mode which still running.

The note that you implemented was usefull ?

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Database Table getting updated after triggering an Idoc
entry.content:
First step is to find the correct location of those data in Function Group, did you perform it, e.g. debug ?

Only if succesful, then start coding

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <xadrc> TYPE adrc_tab.
ASSIGN ('(SAPLSZA0)XADRC') TO <xadrc>.

entry.title: Latest Goal Management - How to report on Backup Goal Plans?
entry.content:
Hi,

after updating to the Latest Goal Management, we face the issue of not being able to report on the Backup Goal Plan. Although the Backup Goal Plan appears in the chooseable templates within Report Center, the report itself does not provide any data on the Backups.

And as the system automatically applies the maxlength attribute to templates that have been converted, we are missing some data that we could like to recover by reporting on the Backups.

Thankful for any ideas.

Cheers

entry.title: Re: MDG document storeage
entry.content:
Hi @isabelle1993 ,

The CR attachment Files are stored in the table USMD1212 in RAWSTRING format.

Best regards,

Aditya

entry.title: Re: Gathering employee data based on the department field.
entry.content: NA

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Data Cloud Dataflow Custom Script
entry.content:
Hello @S_Sarkar 
AFAIK, in CDC dataflow,  it's not possible to SKIP a record using the record.evaluate function and move to the next one! What's your exact use-case, what type of Business Usecase you're trying to achieve?
Feel free to reply for further queries.

Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: Re: Project ID can be Automatic encoding base on project...
entry.content:
You have not mentioned your versions but by using AI4ERP, here are some general steps to review.  Please advise if you need more help:

To configure automatic encoding of Project IDs based on project type or Project Profile in SAP S/4HANA, you generally need to follow these steps in the system's configuration settings. This is a high-level overview and may require administrative access in SAP and consultation of detailed documentation or assistance from an SAP consultant:

1.Access SAP Customizing Implementation Guide (IMG): - Navigate to the SAP IMG by entering the transaction code `SPRO` in the command field. - Click on "SAP Reference IMG" to open the customization options.

2.Define Project Types: - Navigate to `Project System` > `Structures` > `Operate Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)` > `Define Project Types`. - Here you can define different project types which can be later used to categorize projects.

3.Define Number Ranges for Project IDs: - In the same area, you can specify number ranges for different project types. - Navigate to `Project System`, and under `Structures`, you select `Number Ranges for WBS Elements`. - Define number ranges specific to each project type, ensuring each has a unique range.

4.Configure Project Profiles: - Navigate back to `Project System` > `Structures` > `Operate Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)` > `Define Project Profiles`. - Each profile can be linked with a project type to ensure correct settings.

5.Assign Number Ranges to Project Profiles: - Within the same customizing path, assign these number ranges to different project profiles, linking them to the project types.

6.Set Up Automatic Number Assignment: - Under `Project System`, configure the settings for automatic number assignment. - Ensure the system settings allow for automatic assignment and that the logic links the correct number ranges with project types or profiles.

7.Assignment of Project IDs Based on Profile or Type: - Once project types, profiles, and number ranges are defined and linked, the system should automatically generate Project IDs based on the associated type or profile when a new project is created.

8.Testing: - Before rolling out, thoroughly test these configurations in a development or quality assurance environment to ensure accuracy and functionality.

9.Implementation and Training: - Implement the changes in the production environment (if applicable) and ensure users are trained on the new process. Please note: This is a broad outline and the steps might vary slightly based on your specific version of SAP S/4HANA and any customizations your organization has.

entry.title: Re: DMS inspection bath
entry.content: Hi! Thanks a lot for the reply. The solution turned out to be this, when in a DC 10 transaction we combine the object and the screen number (in our case, we use only MARA). When we go to the control batch via QA03, the document number is also displayed there, there was no point in adding a control object.

entry.title: Re: DMS inspection bath
Re: Gathering employee data based on the department field.
entry.content: Thankyou

entry.title: Re: Error Message Type 'W' gets handled as 'I' in BADI
entry.content:
Hello Zhehui,

yes, the "W" is acting like an information. But i think this type of BADI works like this.

I traced the custom logic and found out that it is returned correctly as a W, but in the Sales Order i just get the information back and it gets saved automatically.

Best regards 

 

Filip

entry.title: AIF messages stuck in status 'In processing' though they are successfully processed
entry.content:
Hi There,

Successful AIF messages got stuck in 'In processing' status in case of calling AIF messages in OUTPUT type routine(NACE). But actually they all were successfully processed. Please suggest how to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI/ Integration Suite Splitter returning as repo...
entry.content: I have applied if else logic too after splitter after removing router but issue still same, previous payload getting in response before split value

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion in People Story reports

entry.title: CDS view for Text field within the Customer Material Master Data
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to build a CDS report which has to contain the information from the customer material  (Fiori app : F2499 ), tab "Text". 

I use CDS structure : I_CUSTOMERMATERIAL_2 to get customer material information , but can not find 

text information from this CDS structure.

Can anyone tell me which CDS view contains this text information?

I've searched Customer Material CDS views and I couldn't find it anywhere.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Ad-hoc Service with Fixed Price billing or Billing Plan
entry.content: hi Paolo, Down-payments are not yet supported in Service Orders. This is on the Roadmap and will be included in the Billing Plan functionality.

entry.title: Re: python script that uses oauth2.0 with 'api access' wi...
entry.content:
Someone from SAP support advised us that for this scenario the "SAML2.0 Bearer" choice must be selected under "API Access", instead of "Client Credentials" (which we had tried and got exactly the same behavior that you found).

We have not yet completed the reconfiguration for this "SAML2.0 Bearer" option, because - as far as we understand - it requires establishing "trust" between Datasphere (BTP) and a "trusted identity provider"; see link Add a Trusted Identity Provider in step 6, for option "OAuth2SAMLBearer Principal Propagation Flow".

It is not yet clear to us how to establish this relationship between Datasphere (or via SAP Identity Authentication Service?) and our Microsoft Entra ID. We just asked SAP Support whether they have any detailed documentation for this specific pairing, haven't yet heard back.

 

I am guessing that SAP Support might come back with Simplify SSO with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) & SAP Identity Authentication Service and Integrating the Service with Microsoft Entra ID, however these do not deal directly with Datasphere, instead speak about IAS. Maybe some steps will be the same / similar. Or maybe one does have to set up the trust relationship between SAP IAS and Entra ID first, before Datasphere and its clients can use it. 

Some general Microsoft documentation is at Scenario - Using Microsoft Entra ID to secure access to SAP platforms and applications. Same thrust towards SAP IAS.

entry.title: Re: How to transform yourself from a fresher to an expert SAP Consultant?
entry.content:
Ótimo post! Dicas importantes com links para seguir um método de aprendizado e aperfeiçoamento de carreira no mundo SAP.
Obrigado.

entry.title: Re: Adding Variables to Predictive Analysis
entry.content: Thanks for the answer.  I created an example where I included more past dates. However, I encountered the following message: "Dotacoes_corrigidas is not considered in the forecast due to a lack of future values for dotacoes_corrigidas_acumuladas." Now, I’m able to predict more than one forecast period, but the additional variable I’m adding is still not being considered. Thanks in advance Ganjos

entry.title: Re: QS61 how it works
entry.content: Hello! I'm sorry, maybe I explained it wrong. We have material, for example, 5 pieces, for 5 production order, 1 piece in each order. The material is actually from the same production, these 5 pieces were made at the same time. Sap will create a separate inspection batch for each piece, and we need to combine them into one batch, because otherwise we will have to enter the same thing in 5 batches

entry.title: Re: python script that uses oauth2.0 with 'api access' wi...
entry.content: I'll update once we hear back from SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Which views are available for creating Code List fiel...
entry.content:
I checked AI4ERP to determine what makes a CDS (Core Data Services) view appear in the Value Help View search help within the "Custom Fields and Logic" app in S/4 HANA.   There are some general guidelines based on standard practice:

1.Annotations: Ensure that the CDS view has the necessary annotations. Annotations such as `@ObjectModel.valueHelpDefinition: [ { entity : 'your_valuehelp_entity' } ]` might be required to enable the CDS view as a possible value help. This annotation signals that the CDS is suitable for value help functionality.

2.Interface Views: The CDS view might need to be an interface view or contain specific characteristics that allow it to be used in a user interface scenario.

3.Authorization: Make sure that the CDS view is not restricted by authorization checks that prevent it from being listed in certain contexts. It needs to have proper roles and authorizations to be visible in the UI components.

4.SAP Standards: Adhere to SAP standards and naming conventions for creating CDS views. Sometimes views do not appear if they deviate from expected standards or configurations.

5.Configuration: Check if there are configuration settings at a broader system level that impact which CDS views are available for selection. There might be administrative settings that either restrict or enable views for particular use cases.

entry.title: Active/Active Read enabled option
entry.content:
Dear Team,

our requirement is, customer was using s4hana, they want to connect the secondary system in SAP using read access mode, we have enabled the logreplay_readaccess mode in secondary system, but when we are trying to login it shows license error, i want to know which license is required to login into secondary system, i have found below SAP note, please give me some solution for this requirement.

please go through the link regarding the Active/Active Read enabled option

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4e9b18c116aa42fc84c7dbfd02111aba/6c47a595535e494f94567b209fbf8579.html

2685661 - Licensing required for HANA System Replication

in the license Management what they have mentioned is below

The SAP HANA Active/Active read-enabled option requires additional licensing (see also the Feature Scope Description for SAP HANA); the license for Active/Active (read enabled) also covers the multi-target replication scenario. Active/Active is enabled by setting the system replication operation mode to logreplay_readaccess, the secondary system is then operated automatically with the license key of the primary system. Changes to the license key are done on the primary system and replicated to the secondary system (see also Managing SAP HANA Licenses).

Thanks,

+91-9553836498

entry.title: Re: Project ID can be Automatic encoding base on project...
entry.content: Hi , the Version is SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Calculation logic of moving price
entry.content:
Dear experts,

May i know if you can help to explain me what's the calculation logic of moving price when not relevant for Settlement and material Price control is 'S'？

As per my understanding, if the material which is no movement in the current period, the material's PUP should EQ to last period's PUP, meanwhile the moving price should also be equal to last period's PUP. But in my system environment, I couldn't find a logic to calculate the exact price through the law of historical prices.

Material configuration：Price control'S' & Price Determ'3'

Looking forward your kindly help.

Related t-code: CKM3&MM03

Thanks and best regards,

Charles

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Data Cloud Dataflow Custom Script
entry.content: Thanks for your reply, I want to process only those records which don't have certain value in the custom field. Further, I can ignore those values by using != in the accounts.search API query. But I am afraid != for a custom field may make the search slow. So I want to pull records and further filter it in record.evaluate with an if condition. Could you please suggest any option? or is it okay to have != in the search query for a custom field. I assume custom fields are not indexed in CDC internally.

entry.title: Replays Available: SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024
entry.content:
SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024 took place on November 13 and 14. 

This was an exclusive virtual event designed to provide valuable insights and strategies to elevate your business transformation through SAP’s comprehensive digital support experience. 

If you could not attend the event or would like to review specific sessions, you can access all materials such as replays, presentations, and consolidated FAQs here. 

All sessions are available in multiple languages: English, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese and Japanese. 

 Here is a glimpse of what's available for you: 

  • Discover how SAP is revolutionizing customer support with AI to strengthen your business. 
  • Explore real-time AI applications with Real-Time Support. 
  • Uncover powerful strategies for optimizing cloud and hybrid solutions with SAP Cloud ALM and the SAP Business Transformation Center. 
  • See how SAP Ariba can simplify and streamline procurement processes. 
  • Boost your RISE with SAP project with our tailored SAP Enterprise Support offerings. 
  • Accelerate your SAP Business Technology Platform implementation. 
  • Drive transformation using SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX. 
  • Deepen your understanding of SAP HCM and explore ways to enhance employee engagement.

entry.title: Re: Introducing new type of Data Access Control "Operator and Value" for SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @rmuhuri isn’t that what is expected? Anything in product group range 10 to 20 is permitted and 02 is not in the range and so restricted, unless it is EMEA and GER.

Now we need to start MD04 as an Webgui, through a Fiori Application.

But: No Property Box Values

We just get a Property Box, with Descriptions, we get the right amount of Row's but we do not have any Values in.

 

 

Any Ideas?

entry.title: Re: Freight Order fields are un editable (Truck Info)
entry.content:
Can you also post a screenshot of your item hierarchy? And of Status?

Truck (and Trailer) fields come from items (AVR and PVR Item, respectively), and if blocked, it meas that the respective Items are blocked for editing.

entry.title: Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document App - S4HANA Custom Field Extension
entry.content:
Hello all ,

Clients requirement is to add a  Supplier Material field  within the Items section of "Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document " App, as the arrow in the image indicates.

 

And it is from the entity set “GR4PO_DL_Item” of gateway project MMIM_GR4PO_DL marked in above screenshot from SEGW  and it is not from the CDS view.

 

  1. We started by looking into the issue with Adapt UI to try to add the field that way. However, this did not work because the field does not exist.

 

  1. We tried using Custom Fields. There, we used the business contexts PO Item and Material Document Item. That did not work either.

Is there any way we could introduce the new field “Supplier Material” in the items section of the above app?

 

Please let me know your thoughts. 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Data Maintenance and Cleanup of a SAP Commerce Cloud Project
entry.content:
Hello @Summitt12 

CCv2 is a PaaS not an IaaS. And data cleaning is complex topic. Pure technical data could be managed internally. But technico-functional data, mentioned in this article, must be managed by business administrator. 

By default, SAP Commerce Cloud is providing pre-setup Cronjob/Tools to do this cleaning : e.i. data retention framework

Then, it is the responsibility of business to put in place the rules : which data, how, when, and so on.

entry.title: Re: Customer Proposal Clarification
entry.content:
Dear PCA, 

Please refer to my answers below. 

Answer 1. To execute R&R items (services), you can raise a Service Request (SR) after consulting with the CDM/TSM or dCEM according to your support model. You may need a separate contract to raise a SR and conduct the tasks.

Answer 2.  Information regarding security are provided via SOC 2 report. As it is a customised report that is only provided to the customer directly, you should ask the customer to request a SOC 2 report at SAP Trust Center. 

Answer 3. If the point of the question is "who is responsible for the performance/efficiency of the test and recovery", it is counting on SAP ECS, as SAP ECS is performing the test and recovery. However, it may be also impacted by other variations such as the size of data and approach or timing of recovery - which is decided by the customer or partner.

Answer 4. Please refer to the Microsoft official websites explaining Service Healing. 

Answer 5. It is possible to change, but it will be provided as a charged service. Please reach out to the the dedicated contavt - CDM or dCEM for more information. Any downtime or additional cost may occur.

Answer 6. Please check on this point with ZScaler (If ZScaler is not supporting SUSE Linux, there is a possibility that SAP is also not supported). 

 

Hope this helps. 
Regards, Rin

entry.title: Manual retrigger for messages flowing through TPM
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am currently working on TPM related interface for which I have encountered a message failure.

After 3 retries the message has been moved to dead letter queue, but I am unable to retrigger the message after fixing the error because the status is still in 'waiting' state. 

Getting the error "Message is not in a failed state and cannot be reprocessed" while I try to retrigger the message.

Can anyone provide me the solution on how the message can be reprocessed.

 

Thanks,

Aditya

entry.title: Re: MDG document storeage
entry.content:
Hi 

In SAP, documents related to change requests are typically stored in the SAP Content Server or a linked Document Management System (DMS). The table USMD1211 links documents to change requests, but the physical storage is usually managed externally (e.g., in DMS tables like `DRAD` for attachments). To download documents in bulk, you can use SAP's Archive Link or a custom ABAP program that accesses the DMS content.

you can refer to the following SAP documentation:
SAP Content Server Documentation: http://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_CONTENT_SERVER

SAP Archive Link Documentation: http://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_ARCHIVELINK

SAP Document Management System (DMS): http://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_DOCUMENT_MANAGEMENT_SYSTEM

entry.title: Re: SAC Data Action: Copy data from one quarter to another quarter
Dynamic Input Form and Report
entry.content:
Hi Team, I want to deliver this mini project. The report should include a side-by-side input form and a report, with only date and version selection. Currently, this is designed in Excel. Below are the points that need to be considered before designing. I need your help in modeling and report design.

Input Form

  • Type of Project: This should be a dropdown, and based on the segment master, the input form should change partially, not fully.
  • Asset Name: This should be a text input.
  • Asset Type: This should be a dropdown.

Similarly, the form should be designed accordingly.

Report

As you can see, each cell has a different formula written. I am unable to visualize how to design this.

entry.title: Re: Manual retrigger for messages flowing through TPM
entry.content:
Hi 

To reprocess the message in the dead letter queue, you need to first change its status from 'waiting' to 'failed.' This can often be done through a dedicated admin tool or by manually updating the message state in the database.

Once the status is set to 'failed,' you should be able to retrigger the message for reprocessing. Additionally, ensure there are no locks or dependencies preventing the status update.

entry.title: Service Order with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier
entry.content:
In this blog you will learn about the Service Order process with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier (Intercompany Stock Transport Order). This service process flow is based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, release 2023. 

Step 1: Create and Release Service Order with External Spare Part item 

Start the process by creating a service order with an External Spare Part item. Standard item category SRPM can be used. In the Involved Parties on item level you can enter a prefered vendor and enter an Intercompany Supplier. 

When releasing the service order (and save), a purchase requisition is created. The purchase requisition is visible in the Transaction History. 

In this example we first create a Purchase Requisition. It is also possible to configure the Service Order item category to immediately create a Purchase Order. 

Step 2: Convert Purchase Requisition into Purchase Order

In our example, company code 1710 is requesting this spare part from company code 1010 and plant 1010. A Supplier Business Partner is created for Plant 1010. 

The Purchase Order is created for this Intercompany Supplier and the Purchase Order is account assigned to the Service Order Item. 

Based on the Documentation described here, the Purchase Order type is NB, item category BLANK, and Delivery type NLCC. 

The Purchase Order is also visible in the Transaction History of the Service Order. 

Step 3: Outbound Delivery for Purchase Order

The Intercompany Supplier (Plant 1010) creates an Outbound Delivery based on the Purchase Order (transaction VL10B).

Step 4: Outbound Delivery - Perform Picking and Goods Issue

From the Outbound Delivery, perform picking of the spare parts in Plant 1010 and trigger Goods Issue. Standard movement type 643 is used in our example as a 2-step approach. A separate goods receipt needs to posted in the demanding company (1710).

Step 5: Goods Receipt for Outbound Delivery

Create a Goods Receipt based on the Outbound Delivery (using movement type 101). 

Step 6: Check updates in Service Order

Both goods movements (good issue and goods receipt) are added to the Transaction History of the Service Order.

Step 7: Create Intercompany Invoice

The supplying company (1010) creates an intercompany invoice towards the requesting company (1710).

Step 8: Create and post Vendor Invoice 

In the Requesting Company, create and post the Vendor Invoice from the Intercompany supplier. The vendor invoice is added to the Purchase Order history.

Step 9: Check Margin Analysis for the Service Order

In Margin Analysis for the Service Order item we see the cost postings from the Goods Receipt and correction from the Vendor Invoice.

Step 10: Create Service Confirmation

Now that the Spare Part is available in the Requesting Company, the service technician can create a service confirmation and use the spare part.

Step 11: Create Customer Invoice

When the service is finished and the service order is completed, the customer billing process can be started.

Step 12: Margin Analysis

Finally, check revenue and cost postings in the Margin Analysis for the Service Order.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 SAP Excel AddIn Compatibility

Office 365 is mentioned in the requirement page for excel add-in in the Help.

It is a subscription plan which provides license to latest version of MS office. No need to mention any version specifically.

 

Only desktop version of excel will be supported.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing: Yield Confirmation with Final Confirmation
entry.content:

Could anyone provide the old XSLT to me? If I only activate the Final Confirmation in the current XSLT, it always sends a Final Confirmation, but it only should do this, if the ordered quantity is already confirmed. I hope that this check, if the whole quantity is already confirmed, is included in the old XSLT.

entry.title: SAC: missing updates for KBA 3533963 ? (Table.onSelect not triggered anymore)
entry.content:
Hi,
KBA 3533963 was released on 30.10.2024 and describes that standard events like Table.onSelect are not triggered anymore as they used to be.
in my case I have 2 strories with tables where selection type is set to single cell (as a workaround for open improvement request 280582 ).
Since Q4 update users have to right-click in the table to trigger event .onSelect. Previous to update it was left-click.
will this be solved in the forseable future?
br
edgar

entry.title: Re: No JE for Use tax on Supplier invoice
entry.content:
Hi Chintan, are you using external tax system for tax determination or standard SAP. Also, review your configuration with the Use tax code setup.

Here you can find more information:

External Sales and Use Tax Calculation

Import Tax Codes

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [3]: Creating events with deep payloads
entry.content:
Hi @MioYasutake 

triggering the event is always extremely dependent on your use case and where you want to trigger the event from. 

The following is an example where I use a local event handler to catch an event and trigger another custom one. Local event handlers are not intended for that, but it still works :D.

Global class:

CLASS z_local_bp_event_consumption DEFINITION PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL FOR EVENTS OF I_BusinessPartnertp_2 .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS z_local_bp_event_consumption IMPLEMENTATION.
ENDCLASS.

Local types:

CLASS lcl_abap_behv_event_handler DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT changed_instances FOR BusinessPartner~Changed.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_abap_behv_event_handler IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD consume_changed.

    cl_abap_tx=>save( ).

    LOOP AT changed_instances INTO DATA(changed_instance).

        DATA(et_events) = VALUE zbp_addr_changed=>tt_events(
        (
            businesspartner = '101'
            partnerId = '222'
            firstName = 'Martin'
            lastName = 'Lopas'
            businessPartnerType = '1'
            address = VALUE #(
                nation = ''
                city = ''
                street = 'Church Street 103'
                houseNumber = ''
                postalCode = '56265'
                country = 'US'
            )
        )
    ).

    zbp_addr_changed=>raise_address_changed( it_events = et_events ).

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

The event in this example uses a slightly different payload, but other that that the setup is exactly the same.

 

One reason why your event doesn't show in the event monitor could be because there is no topic binding for that event. The event monitor only shows events with a topic binding on that channel

entry.title: Re: How to copy from actuals and budget to forecast in sa...
entry.content:
Hi @abe_enoch

The Date dimension member ID (ex, 202410) is not a numeric value but a string. Therefore, the date dimension member ID cannot use comparison operators, nor can addition or subtraction operations be performed. The date dimension member ID needs to be converted into a numeric value to perform comparison operators and addition or subtraction operations. One way to transform it into a unique month number is by using the formula:

  • @Month = (YEAR([d/Date])) * 12 + MONTH([d/Date])
  • Example
String Numeric  Numeric  Numeric 
Date member ID YEAR([d/Date])  Month([d/Date]) (YEAR([d/Date])) * 12 + MONTH([d/Date]) 
202401                    2,0241                                                                 24,289
202402                    2,0242                                                                 24,290
202403                    2,0243                                                                 24,291
202404                    2,0244                                                                 24,292
202405                    2,0245                                                                 24,293
202406                    2,0246                                                                 24,294
202407                    2,0247                                                                 24,295
202408                    2,0248                                                                 24,296

 

I hope this makes it clear.

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to uninstall /all on "On installation...
entry.content:
Hello, you, can’t do it by adding a script to "On installation Start" explanation here:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sapsetup-how-do-i-call-uninstall-sapgui-32bit-version/qaq-p/12764158/comment-id/4791651

What you can do is creating bat file and entering following 2 lines, one for Uninstall and second for install package e.g. "GUISAPx64".

\\YourSAPInstallServer\SAPInstall\Setup\NwSapSetup.exe /all /nodlg /Uninstall

\\YourSAPInstallServer\SAPInstall\Setup\NwSapSetup.exe /nodlg /Package="GUISAPx64"

regards Damian

entry.title: Powerdesigner: Create calculated collection based on attribute
entry.content:
Hi all,

This seems like kind of a basic question, but I haven't been able to find a whole lot about calculated collections.

I'm trying to create a calculated collection of objects in SAP PowerDesigner, so I can use this pre-filtered set of entities in my report. The problem is: I can't get the syntax right. The best result I've gotten so far (in the report) is a number of lists containing one entity each instead of one list containing every entity. In the dependencies tab, only one entity (the entity whose property window is open) is visible. 

This is the code I used:

Function %Collection%(obj, coll)
   If obj.GetAttribute("Stereotype") = "Relatie" Then
   coll.Add obj
   End if
   %Collection% = True
   
End Function

Can the calculated collection be used as a pre-defined filter for entities (so only entities with the stereotype "Relatie" are in it)?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Saprouter denies https after some time
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a pretty straightforward saprouter config file.
P * * *
and some lines
P <ip> website.domain 443

It works just fine, BUT after a few days the router starts denying https access.
I looked at all logs, nothing restarted the router or renewed its access table.

If I commit: saprouter -n
It is fine again and it is working fine for a few days.

I installed the latest version, but the problem persists.

What could be the reason?

Thanks!
I.G.

Joern

entry.title: Re: IT0006 Postal code activation for Switzerland country
entry.content: Added S/4HANA tag, please remove tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Localization.

entry.title: Re: LIMITATION OF FORM TEMPLATE CUSTOMIZATION
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please mention what exact area do you mean?

Best Regards,
Owen
